steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify bc/ab as a sine ratio in triangle ABC
steps.content: Triangle ABC has a right angle at C and angle A = 32°. The ratio $\frac{bc}{ab} = \frac{BC}{AB}$ is the side opposite angle A divided by the hypotenuse. In a right triangle, this equals $\sin(32^{\circ})$.

steps.title: Recognize the complementary angle relationship
steps.content: In triangle DEF, the right angle is at F and angle D = 58°. The angles 32° and 58° are complementary (they sum to 90°). By the cofunction identity, $\sin(32^{\circ}) = \cos(58^{\circ})$.

steps.title: Find the matching cosine ratio in triangle DEF
steps.content: In right triangle DEF with angle D = 58°, the cosine of this angle (adjacent over hypotenuse) is $\frac{DF}{DE} = \frac{df}{de}$. Therefore, $\frac{bc}{ab} = \frac{df}{de}$, which matches the listed ratio $df / de$, so B is correct.
